channel.title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: U.S. Transfers Thousands of ISIS Prisoners to Iraq From Syria
item.description: The moves appear to highlight lingering doubts in Washington about the new Syrian government’s ability to ensure security.

item.title: Trump’s FTC chief says he’s not the ‘speech police,’ but reality has too liberal a bias on Apple News app
item.description: Andrew Ferguson urged Cook to review what is used on the Apple News feed and take corrective action.

item.title: Trump is making foreign tourism great again. How much will he hurt the World Cup?
item.description: While the rest of the world saw a travel bump in 2025, with global international arrivals up 4%, the U.S. saw a downturn

item.title: Goldman Sachs General Counsel Kathryn Ruemmler Resigns Over Epstein Ties
item.description: Kathryn Ruemmler, a former top Obama administration lawyer, is out at Goldman Sachs after emails showed a friendship with the sex offender Jeffrey Eps...

item.title: San Francisco Teachers End Strike After 4 Days
item.description: Public schools are expected to reopen on Wednesday for 50,000 students in the city. Teachers demanded higher wages and health care benefits.

item.title: What It Means to Be a White ‘Race Traitor’
item.description: From Schwerner and Goodman to Good and Pretti, white people putting themselves in harm’s way has helped galvanize Americans for justice.

item.title: You love your dog too much. Blame the broken American Dream and loss of purpose since the pandemic
item.description: Dogs aren’t just being used as a substitute for people. I believe Americans are turning to dogs to alleviate the erosion of social life itself.

item.title: How The Times Is Digging Into Millions of Pages of Epstein Files
item.description: Two dozen journalists. A pile of pages that would reach the top of the Empire State Building. And an effort to find the next revelation in a sprawling...

item.title: Politicians scramble on data centers after putting their voters on the hook for Big Tech’s job-killing AI efforts
item.description: “What do you do when Big Tech, because of the very profitable nature of these data centers, can simply outbid grandma for power in the short run?”

item.title: Valentine’s Day office etiquette: Experts share the 80:20 rule for talking about your significant other at work—and the 3 times when it’s never okay
item.description: While Hallmark may want you to confess your undying love for your partner, perhaps these declarations are best left inside a card—and well away from t...

item.title: 2 ISIS Supporters Who Planned Attack on Jews in U.K. Get Life Sentences
item.description: The case underlines how the Islamic State has seized on rising antisemitism provoked by the Gaza war to radicalize and encourage would-be assailants.

item.title: Live Updates: German Leader Says International Order ‘No Longer Exists’
item.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz of Germany, speaking at the Munich Security Conference, criticized President Trump’s rapid reorientation of American foreign...

item.title: U.S. Transfers Thousands of ISIS Prisoners to Iraq From Syria
item.description: The moves appear to highlight lingering doubts in Washington about the new Syrian government’s ability to ensure security.

item.title: Japan Seizes Chinese Fishing Boat; Move Likely to Add to Tensions
item.description: The seizure of the trawler, which Japan said was sailing in its waters in the East China Sea, is likely to add to tensions between Tokyo and Beijing.
